description.content: TLS certificate information endpoints.
Lists all loaded TLS certificates including file name, serial number, subject alternate names, and days until expiration in JSON format.
Cluster management and information endpoints.

Lists all configured cluster manager clusters including all discovered upstream hosts in each cluster along with per-host statistics. Provides information about the health status and connection metrics of upstream hosts.

query.description: Output format for cluster information.

description.content: Configuration inspection and dump endpoints.
Returns the currently loaded configuration in JSON format, including bootstrap, clusters, endpoints, listeners, scoped routes, routes, secrets, and ECDS filter configurations.

query.description: Filter the configuration dump to only return the specified resource type.
Field mask to apply to the configuration dump, limiting which fields are returned.
Regular expression to filter the returned configuration by resource name.
Include EDS configuration in the dump.

description.content: Returns the unmodified configuration as it was loaded during the initialization process.

query.description: Field mask to apply to the initialization dump.

description.content: Debugging and traffic inspection endpoints.
Installs a traffic tap configuration that allows inspecting traffic matching specific filter rules. The tap configuration is supplied in the request body.

response.name: Tap configuration installed and streaming tap results.

item.name: Envoy Proxy Get Mutex Contention Stats

request.name: Envoy Proxy Get Mutex Contention Stats

description.content: Dumps current Envoy mutex contention stats in JSON format, if mutex tracing is enabled.
General admin interface endpoints.
Returns the admin interface home page with links to all available endpoints.

response.name: Successful response with admin home page.

description.content: Returns a list of all available admin endpoints and their descriptions.

response.name: Successful response with list of endpoints.

description.content: Health check management endpoints.
Returns a 200 OK response if the server has been initialized and is ready to accept traffic. Returns 503 if the server is not yet initialized.
Causes the server to fail inbound health checks. This requires the use of the HTTP health check filter. Useful for draining a server prior to shutting it down or performing a full restart.
Negates the effect of POST /healthcheck/fail, causing the server to resume passing inbound health checks.
Listener management endpoints.
Lists all configured listeners, including their names and the addresses they are listening on.

query.description: Output format for listener information.

description.content: Drains all listeners, causing them to stop accepting new connections. Existing connections are allowed to complete.

query.description: When set, listeners are drained gracefully with a period where both old and new listeners accept connections.
Drain only inbound listeners and leave outbound listeners intact.

description.content: Logging level management endpoints.

item.name: Envoy Proxy View or Modify Log Levels

request.name: Envoy Proxy View or Modify Log Levels

description.content: Enable or change logging levels at runtime. Without query parameters, returns the current logging levels. With a query parameter, sets the logging level for a specific logger or all loggers.

query.description: Set the log level for all loggers. Valid values include trace, debug, info, warning/warn, error, critical, and off.
Set a glob pattern for file path to filter the log output.

description.content: CPU and heap profiling endpoints.

item.name: Envoy Proxy Enable or Disable CPU Profiler

request.name: Envoy Proxy Enable or Disable CPU Profiler

description.content: Enables or disables the CPU profiler. Requires compilation with gperftools. The output file can be then analyzed with pprof.

query.description: Enable (y) or disable (n) the CPU profiler.

item.name: Envoy Proxy Enable or Disable Heap Profiler

request.name: Envoy Proxy Enable or Disable Heap Profiler

description.content: Enables or disables the heap profiler. Requires compilation with gperftools.

query.description: Enable (y) or disable (n) the heap profiler.

description.content: Runtime configuration management endpoints.
Returns the current runtime settings, including any overrides that have been applied via the admin interface or runtime layer configuration.
Adds or modifies runtime values as passed in query parameters. To delete a previously added override, use an empty value for the parameter.

query.description: Key-value pairs to set as runtime overrides. Multiple parameters can be passed.

description.content: Server information and lifecycle endpoints.
Returns information about the running Envoy server including version, uptime, command line options, and hot restart version.
Returns current memory allocation and heap usage in bytes, providing insights into memory consumption of the Envoy process.

item.name: Envoy Proxy Get Hot Restart Version

request.name: Envoy Proxy Get Hot Restart Version

description.content: Returns the hot restart compatibility version. This can be matched against the output of the --hot-restart-version command line option to determine whether a new binary and the running binary are hot restart compatible.

response.name: Successful response with hot restart version.

description.content: Cleanly exits the Envoy server process.
Statistics and metrics endpoints.
Returns statistics that Envoy has collected, including counters, gauges, and histograms. Stats are provided as key-value pairs using a hierarchical dotted notation.

query.description: Output format for the statistics.
Regular expression to filter the returned statistics by name.
Only return statistics that have been written to by Envoy.
Control histogram output mode.

item.name: Envoy Proxy Get Statistics in Prometheus Format

request.name: Envoy Proxy Get Statistics in Prometheus Format

description.content: Returns statistics in Prometheus exposition format suitable for scraping by a Prometheus server.

query.description: Only return statistics that have been written to by Envoy.
Include text readout gauges in the output.
Regular expression to filter the returned statistics by name.

item.name: Envoy Proxy Get Recent Stat Lookups

request.name: Envoy Proxy Get Recent Stat Lookups

description.content: Returns a table of recently looked-up stat names.

response.name: Successful response with recent stat lookups.

description.content: Resets all counters to zero. This is useful when used in conjunction with GET /stats during debugging sessions.

The Envoy Proxy Administration Interface provides a local HTTP-based management API for querying and modifying various aspects of the Envoy server at runtime. It serves as a critical operational tool for monitoring, debugging, and managing Envoy proxy instances. The admin interface typically runs on port 9901 by default. All mutation operations must be sent as HTTP POST requests; GET requests will not perform changes. Note that this endpoint is not authenticated, so access should be restricted in production environments.
